thig6.1:1.1: [Paṭācārā recounts the Buddha’s words:] “You don’t know the path
thig6.1:1.3: that being who has come from where?—
thig6.1:1.4: the one you lament as ‘my son.’
thig6.1:2.1: But when you know the path
thig6.1:2.4: for that is the nature of beings.
thig6.1:3.2: Without permission, he went from here
thig6.1:3.5: And coming one way from here,
thig6.1:3.6: he goes yet another from there.
thig6.1:4.3: As he came, so he has gone—
thig6.1:4.4: so what is there to lament?”
thig6.1:5.2: the arrow so hard to see, embedded in my heart, he [the Buddha] expelled from me
thig6.1:6.3: to the Buddha, Dhamma, & Saṅgha I go,
